title: Comb-shaped High Molecular Weight Polyether Consisting of Oxyethylene Units for Polymer Solid Electrolyte
abstract:
Polymer International 43 (1997) 269È273
Comb-shaped High Molecular Weight
Polyether Consisting of Oxyethylene
Units for Polymer Solid Electrolyte
Y. Ikeda,*1 H. Masui,1 S. Syoji,2 T. Sakashita,2 Y. Matoba2 & S. Kohjiya3
1 Faculty of Engineering and Design, Kyoto Institute of Technology, Matsugasaki, Sakyo, Kyoto 606, Japan
2 Daiso Co., Ltd, Otakasu, Amagasaki, Hyogo 660, Japan
3 Institute for Chemical Research, Kyoto University, Gokasyo, Uji, Kyoto 611, Japan
(Received 24 October 1996; revised version received 3 January 1997; accepted 22 January 1997)
Abstract: Novel high molecular weight comb-shaped polyethers were synthe-
sized and used as the matrix of a polymer solid electrolyte. Both the main chain
and the side chain of these polyethers consist of oxyethylene units. The new
polyethers possess Ðlm-forming properties, because the weight-average molecular
weights were over 106. The short side chains of oxyethylene units gave rise to less
crystallization of poly(oxyethylene) segments and to an increase of ionic conduc-
tivity when doped with lithium perchlorate.
Polym. Int. 43, 269È273 (1997)
No. of Figures: 3.
No of Tables: 3.
No. of Refs: 13
Key words: polymer
solid
electrolyte,
oxyethylene
segment,
comb-shaped
polymer, high molecular weight, ionic conductivity
INTRODUCTION
Polymer solid electrolytes1,2 have been paid much
attention because of their importance for electronic
devices, especially chemical batteries. A polymer solid
electrolyte is expected to be of light weight and able to
be easily formed into thin Ðlms. Thus thin and light
weight batteries of high performance can be fabricated,
once an excellent polymer solid electrolyte is obtained
having high conductivity and good durability, as well as
the capability of industrial production.
Elastomeric solid electrolytes have some advantages
over inorganic and polymeric (polymers of higher glass
transition temperature,
than room temperature)
Tg ,
solid electrolytes. Suitable elasticity can result in Ñat,
thin and Ñexible solid electrolytes, which would be very
useful for manufacturing paper-like batteries and elec-
trochromic displays.3 Elasticity can also give excellent
contact between an electrolytic layer and an electrode in
chemical batteries.
* To whom all correspondence should be addressed.
The authors have been working on elastomeric
materials, judging that an elastomer could be the best
choice as a matrix for ion conduction,3 because the
rubbery state is actually a liquid from a rheological
viewpoint, even though it is classiÐed as a solid from its
appearance.4 An elastomer may, therefore, be regarded
as a polymeric “solventÏ, and a higher conductivity of
ions would be expected in the matrix than in the glassy
or crystalline state. Because all the ions are much larger
than electrons, which are the carriers in electronic con-
ductors, mobility of ions in the matrix has to be con-
sidered Ðrst. For example, poly(oxyethylene)-grafted
poly(siloxane),3
segmented
polyurethaneureas
with
poly(oxyethylene) È poly(oxytetramethylene) È poly(oxy -
ethylene) soft segments5 and poly(ethylene oxide-co-epi-
chlorohydrin)s6 were studied as elastomeric solid elec-
trolytes.
As in these studies, up to now poly(oxyethylene)
(PEO) has been considered as one component for
polymer solid electrolytes, and many studies on PEO-
based polymeric solid electrolytes have been carried
out.1,2,7 Alkaline salts are easily dissolved in PEO and
269
1997 SCI. Polymer International 0959-8103/97/$17.50
Printed in Great Britain
(
 270
Y . Ikeda et al.
Fig. 1. Synthesis of comb-shaped polyether consisting of
oxyethylene units.
the alkaline metal ions are complexed with polyether
oxygen. Additionally, the efficiency of ion hopping in
the PEO matrix is high.1,2,8 However, a big disadvan-
tage of PEO is that it is crystalline polymer. The crys-
talline part does not contribute to ionic conduction at
all, which causes a decrease of ionic conductivity. How
can the crystallinity of PEO segments be prevented?
This is the key to developing a highly functional PEO-
based polymeric solid electrolyte.
A speciality rubber is a copolymer of ethylene oxide
(EO) and epichlorohydrin. By the polymerization of EO
with epichlorohydrin, the crystallinity of the oxyethy-
lene segments in the copolymer becomes small or disap-
pears, and consequently the copolymer is used as an
elastomer. We had already studied poly(ethylene oxide-
co-epichlorohydrin) as a polymeric solid electrolyte and
found it to show good ionic conductivity.6 To develop a
better polymer solid electrolyte, we carried out a molec-
ular design and evaluated the characteristics of new
elastomeric materials for this purpose.6
We have combined the advantages of an elastomer,
oxyethylene segments and a comb-shaped polymer in
the molecular design of a polymer solid electrolyte. The
high
molecular
weight
comb-shaped
polyether,9,10
which consisted of oxyethylene units, was assumed to be
an excellent ion conduction matrix. Both the main
chain and the side chains consisted of oxyethylene units,
as shown in Fig. 1. Oxyethylene segments in the side
chain were expected to decrease the crystallization of
the PEO-based polymers. This high molecular weight
copolyether (BEC), was synthesized by coordination
anionic copolymerization of EO and 1,2-epoxy-4,7,10-
trioxaundecane (ETU-2). Watanabe et al. have already
used ETU-2 for the synthesis of an oligomeric macro-
monomer and evaluated the ionic conductivity of the
networks prepared from it.11 However, the polymer net-
works possess crosslinks, which decrease the mobility of
polymer chains and result in relatively low ionic con-
ductivity. Our novel copolymers are expected to show
higher ionic conductivity than the network reported by
Watanabe et al., because they do not need to be cross-
linked, their high molecular weight giving physical sta-
bility.
EXPERIMENTAL
Preparation of polymer solid electrolyte
High molecular weight polyethers (BECs) were synthe-
sized from EO and ETU-2, as shown in Fig. 1, at Daiso
Co., Ltd. PEO was a commercial material from Sumi-
tomo Seika Co., Ltd. Molecular weights and the codes
for the polyethers are shown in Table 1. The number in
the sample code shows the percentage of ETU-2 in the
copolymer. Size exclusion chromatography (SEC) mea-
surement
was
performed
on

using
dimethylformamide
with

weight-average molecular weight
the number-

and the polydispersity
(M1 n)
index
were calculated based on the calibration
(M1 w/M1 n)
of columns using polystyrene standards. Four columns
of Shodex (KD-807, 806, 806M and 803) were used. The
copolymer composition was determined by 1H NMR
measurement on a JEOL-JNM-GSX-270 from Nihon
Denshi Co., Ltd. Deuterated benzene was used as a
solvent.
The copolymers were puriÐed by a modiÐed Soxhlet
extraction method using distilled water for 5 h and dried
at 60¡C under a high vacuum to constant weight. The
polyethers and reagent grade
which were

stored in a desiccator under reduced pressure over blue
silica gel, were dried again under a high vacuum at 60¡C
for 48 h before use.

Comb-shaped polyether for polymer solid electrolyte
Polymer solid electrolytes were prepared from the
polyether were dissolved in anhydrous tetrahydrofuran
(THF), which had been distilled in the presence of
lithium aluminium hydride before use, to form a viscous
homogeneous solution. The polymer solution was cast
on a TeÑonR mould in the desiccator at 40¡C for several
hours, followed by the removal of solvent under a high
vacuum at 60¡C for 3 days. The last number in the
sample codes “BEC-5-3Ï and “PEO-5Ï is the percentage of
lithium ion related to oxygen content (Table 2).
The di†erential scanning calorimetry (DSC) measure-

and a second measurement was carried out after cooling
the sample to c. [110¡C at a rate of c. [10¡C min~1.
The dynamic modulus (E@) and loss tangent (tan d) were
measured by a FT Rheospectra DVE-V4 (Reorogi Co.,
Kyoto) in a tensile mode over the temperature range
[100¡C to c. 60¡C at a heating rate of 2¡C min~1. The
impedance measurements with platinum blocking elec-

High molecular weight comb-shaped polyethers
In the rubber industry, copolymers of EO and epi-
chlorohydrin are synthesized by coordination anionic
polymerization using a Vandenberg-type catalyst,12 and
the molecular weight is very high. The new comb-
shaped polyethers (BECs) were prepared by the same
their composition. The composition was determined
from the integral ratio of the methylene protons of the
oxyethylene segments and the methyl protons of the
side segments in the 1H NMR spectra. The
BEC-5 and BEC-9 was higher than 106. Because of the
entanglement of the long chains of BEC, stable Ðlms
could be prepared by casting from the THF solution of
the polymers, with and without salt. BECs were com-
up to the concentration of [Li]/

BEC holds its shape as an elastomer at room tem-
perature without crosslinking.

markedly in the presence of salt.
Ionic conductivity as a polymer solid electrolyte
In this study, the ionic conductivity was evaluated by
the complex impedance method. When an alternating
polymer solid electrolyte, the ColeÈCole plot generally
possesses a minimum ZA (ZA is an imaginary part of the
complex impedance under the applied electric Ðeld) and
its value was used to determine the ionic conductivity of
the polymer solid electrolyte. The results of the ionic
conductivity measurements at room temperature for the
BEC and PEO Ðlms with salt are summarized in Table
3. These values are some of the highest conductivities so
far reported for polymer solid electrolytes.
Unfortunately, the ionic conductivity at higher tem-
peratures could not be measured, because the minimum
values of ZA in the ColeÈCole plots were not detected.
The reason for this is not yet clear, but most probably
the softening of the BEC Ðlms at higher temperature
may be a cause, since these polymer solid electrolytes
values of c. 38 to 47¡C. The direct sandwiching
of the Ðlm between platinum blocking electrodes was
used for these measurements, and the Ðlm was slightly
compressed in the cell, resulting in more thinning
Comb-shaped polyether for polymer solid electrolyte
TABLE 3. Ionic conductivity of the polyether films complexed with LiClO4
during the measurements at higher temperatures. There-
fore, a TeÑonR ring spacer will be used for future
ionic measurements in the higher temperature region in
order to avoid the dimensional change of this polymer
Non-crosslinked and high molecular weight comb-
shaped polyethers, the main chain and the side chain of
which both consisted of oxyethylene units, were used as
a matrix for a polymer solid electrolyte. This molecular
architecture e†ectively decreased the crystallization of
poly(oxyethylene) segments, though it was not prohibi-
ted completely. The entanglements due to the high
molecular weight of the polymers were found to give an
elastomeric solid state at room temperature without
crosslinking. The composition of the BECs and the
length of side chains will be changed in further research
on higher performance polyethers, which includes using
them as a matrix for ionic conduction.
This research was partly supported by Grant-in-Aid
for Science Research on Priority Areas from the Japa-
Culture. The research number was 269/08229233.
